title: Formula with multiple mirrored values
excerpt:
Monday really needs to support Formulas with multiple mirrored values! 
If you create such a formula, you are returned an error “Formula with multiple mirrored values will be available soon”, however there is no indicati…

posts.author: charlieg (Charlie Gilbert)
posts.cookedHtml:
Monday really needs to support Formulas with multiple mirrored values!

If you create such a formula, you are returned an error “Formula with multiple mirrored values will be available soon”, however there is no indication I can find anywhere on if this is even on the roadmap, or when it is expected to be available.

Not having this feature causes a LOT of manual work on our end, reviewing multiple columns to determine if an item is ready for the next stage in our workflow.

posts.author: murtazamazhar (Murtaza Mazhar)
posts.cookedHtml: Facing the same issue and would love to get some feedback on this.

posts.author: masoudsnez (Masoud Nezami)
posts.cookedHtml: Definitely, it can reduce the extra effort of reviewing multiple columns and boards. It would be a great addition.

posts.author: Ariyota (Ariyo Taghizadeh)
posts.cookedHtml: We have the same problem and Formulas with multiple problem would address most of these issues.

posts.author: jyuc (Jeff)
posts.cookedHtml: Somehow I remembered it was working at one point before. Can anyone confirm?

posts.author: elias.toledo (Elias Toledo)
posts.cookedHtml: I have facing the same problem, what I want to know is when a Team Member will give a solution for us?

posts.author: jholmes (Justin Holmes)
posts.cookedHtml: Also very disappointed this doesn’t work correctly - preventing us from summarizing many items into one value to dashboard.

posts.author: xandr (Xander)
posts.cookedHtml: Still says “coming soon”. Any updates? At least a rough date estimate? Dont know how many months “soon” implies.

posts.author: jholmes (Justin Holmes)
posts.cookedHtml: I submitted a ticket related to this to see if I would have to give up or find a work around, and they said there is “no eta” for this feature being completed, which means they’re not actively working on it.

posts.author: Bill_In_Ohio (Bill Duncan)
posts.cookedHtml: Yes, I agree - this is a needed function. Just creating a simple formula for listing attendees and their companies is a huge hassle. Ideally having them linked to a master table would be the ideal, and then then just being able to do something like FOR EACH {attendee} in {column} CONCAT {attendee}, ", ", {company} can’t be done, let alone where one might want to do more complex calculations.

posts.author: JackNagy (Jack Nagy)
posts.cookedHtml: Looking for this, too

posts.author: ldebernardini (Luigi De Bernardini)
posts.cookedHtml: Same here! Very needed!

posts.author: Rremi (Remi)
posts.cookedHtml:
This is the single most important issue that I’m having at the moment.

It’s like if Excel only allowed you to reference to one other cell. It makes simple processes very cumbersome and needlessly complicated.

posts.author: bobman97 (william)
posts.cookedHtml:
Not sure how to vote but I’d like to inform that we also requrie this.
More specifically to grab how many of a specific item in a mirrored status and use in our formula

SUM(MULTIPLY(count(WHERE {mirror} IS “Tomatoes”), 4), MULTIPLY(count(WHERE {mirror} IS “Cucumber”), 2)

i

posts.author: jholmes (Justin Holmes)
posts.cookedHtml:
I found a workaround that may help for some cases of this issue.

My setup was like this:

Board 1 - Has items with various types (defined by a status column) and values (numbers column).
Board 2 - Summary board for Board 1 - Has one item for each type on Board 1, connects to each item of that type and mirrors their values as a sum.
Board 3 - Summary board for Board 2 - A further summary board to combine certain types from Board 2 for dashboarding.

Essentially, Board 3 is showing a mirror of a mirror of integers.

The issue: trying to use the results in the mirrored columns in Board 3 in formula columns results in an error.
The solution: create a formula column on Board 2 that only contains the value of the desired mirror column, and mirror that formula column on Board 3 instead.

For example, if your mirror column was named “Mirror Cost” your formula column would be called something like Formula Mirror Cost, and contain only {Mirror Cost}. Then on your high level summary board you’d mirror Formula Mirror Cost. Now you can use that mirror column in formulas as normal.

Hope this helps someone!

posts.author: tsims (Tres)
posts.cookedHtml:
Just thought I would add my agreement on this topic. Not being able to use formulas for mirrored values is incredibly cumbersome, and makes it impossible to chart information as well.

This seems like a feature that should be high priority for the devs.

posts.author: FrancisElliott (Francis Elliott)
posts.cookedHtml:
Can you please provide an example of this working? This does not currently appear to be working for me.

I’d also add my support for this feature. It would be super useful and has lots of common use cases.

posts.author: charlieg (Charlie Gilbert)
posts.cookedHtml: It is not working. Monday said the feature was coming a few years ago but it has never worked.

posts.author: FrancisElliott (Francis Elliott)
posts.cookedHtml:
Something fishy going on here:

Screenshot 2023-03-24 at 00.32.212186×828 42.8 KB

In each set of columns, the Formula is just referencing the Mirror directly. And all 3 Mirror columns are referencing the same formula column. However in some cases it works, while in others it doesn’t. It seems possible that certain connection columns are outdated and do not support this functionality.

So the good news is that it does appear to work. But it would be good if we could get some clearer guidelines. It seems as though older columns are not working as effectively for this.

Can someone from Monday shed some light on this?

posts.author: FrancisElliott (Francis Elliott)
posts.cookedHtml: Update: I got this to work consistently. You need to assign a mirrored value from every board where this is connected. So it appears this workaround of using formula columns is working. I recommend!

posts.author: chestersia (Chester Sia)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi Francis, is there a way you can demonstrate to us how you did it? So we can do a temporary work around while we wait for this feature to be implemented.

posts.author: Business_Automated (Greg)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi, I wanted to point to this solution here:

When can we have support for "Formulas with multiple mirrored values? Platform discussions
There is a bit of a cheat code to it… Let’s say we have three boards (A,B, and C). Boards A & B are connected. Boards B & C are connected. Board B rolls up/mirrors information from board A. Board C then mirrors the mirror column on board B of board A’s column. Board C wants to use Board A mirrors in a formula. This is where the error occurs!!! Solution, make this change instead: Boards A & B are connected. Boards B & C are connected. Board B rolls up/mirrors information from board A. …

posts.author: MitchB (Mitch Blobstein)
posts.cookedHtml: I agree - this is very frustrating
